svg.title: One decision core, two transport edges, and a JWKS issuer boundary
svg.desc: At the top are two clients: the browser (MVC web and admin) on the left, and the native mobile app on the right. Both route their login through one central Auth core — MediatR handlers over ASP.NET Core Identity — which makes a single decision: verify the credential, validate the Google token, check lockout, issue. The core then hands each edge the session artifact safest for its medium: the browser edge gets an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookie (no browser-JavaScript token store, so it already has the Backend-for-Frontend property); the native edge gets a bearer JWT plus a rotating refresh token. Across the bottom is the JWKS issuer boundary — asymmetric ES256 signing plus a dot-well-known slash jwks.json discovery endpoint — which lets each host validate a token by the issuer's public key instead of a shared secret, so a drop-in OIDC server later is a config change rather than a rewrite. Hover or focus any node to read it.

g.text: one decision · two edges by medium · one JWKS trust boundary

g.aria-label: Browser client: the server-rendered MVC web app and admin, both routing through the core and keeping no token in browser JavaScript
g.title: Browser · MVC web + admin (no browser-JS token store)

g.aria-label: Native mobile client: the Flutter app, a public OAuth client per RFC 8252
g.title: Native mobile · Flutter (RFC 8252 public client)

g.aria-label: Auth core: MediatR handlers over ASP.NET Core Identity — the single place that decides who you are
g.title: Auth core · MediatR over Identity — one decision
g.text: one decision: verify · validate · lockout · issue
a bug fixed here fixes web + mobile at once

g.aria-label: Browser artifact: an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookie — safe from XSS token theft
g.title: HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite cookie
g.text: BFF property for free · no XSS token surface

g.aria-label: Native artifact: a bearer JWT access token plus a rotating refresh token, held in the app
g.title: bearer JWT + rotating refresh token
g.text: held in the app · never in a browser

g.aria-label: JWKS issuer boundary: asymmetric ES256 signing plus a jwks.json discovery endpoint, so each host validates by public key not shared secret — the seam that makes a drop-in OIDC server a config change, not a rewrite
g.title: JWKS issuer boundary — validate by public key, not a shared secret
g.text: each host validates by the issuer's public key, not a shared secret
the trust seam — a drop-in OIDC server later is a config change, not a rewrite
